image_manifest.title: Free Vibrations of Ring- and Stringer-Stiffened Conical Shells
image_manifest.journal_hint: J. Acoust. Soc. Am., Vol. 46, No. 1 (Part 2), 1969 (page 176 visible on IMG-002)

manifest_summary.total_images_note: 30 original images (IMG-001..IMG-030) + 1 user-supplied insertion (IMG-026B) added in revision r2 to supply the previously-missing Appendix Eq. (A4)/H_4. Original numbering and batch structure are UNCHANGED; the insertion uses a suffixed ID so no downstream ID is shifted.
manifest_summary.revision: r2 (2026-06-09): inserted IMG-026B (Appendix Eq. A4 / H_4) between IMG-026 and IMG-027; resolved flag MF-001.
manifest_summary.images_per_batch: 5 (batch F now holds 6: IMG-026, IMG-026B, IMG-027, IMG-028, IMG-029, IMG-030)
manifest_summary.apparent_document_order: IMG-001..IMG-030 appear to be in natural paper reading order: front matter (title/abstract, symbol list, introduction), Section I Theory (Eqs 1-33, Figs 1-3), Section II Experimental (Tables I-II, Fig 4), Section III Results (Figs 5,7,9,11,12 + discussion), Appendix A (Eqs A1-A12, IN1-IN16 definitions).
manifest_summary.visible_equation_range: Eq. (1) through Eq. (33); Appendix Eqs (A1)-(A12). Eq. (A4) now supplied via inserted IMG-026B (no gap remains).
manifest_summary.visible_tables: Table I (Shell dimensions), Table II (Shell material properties)
manifest_summary.visible_figures: Fig. 1, Fig. 2, Fig. 3, Fig. 4, Fig. 5, Fig. 7, Fig. 9, Fig. 11, Fig. 12

manifest_level_flags.flag: ORIGINAL ISSUE: Appendix equation (A4) / coefficient H_4 was MISSING from the image set. IMG-026 ended at Eq. (A3) (H_3); IMG-027 began at Eq. (A5) (H_5, confirmed by zoom of the left margin showing "H_5 = D_w pi sin a (IN13..."). RESOLUTION (r2): The user supplied the missing screenshot, now catalogued as IMG-026B, inserted between IMG-026 and IMG-027. IMG-026B contains Eq. (A4)/H_4 AND also repeats Eq. (A5)/H_5 (which independently appears on IMG-027) - the (A5) overlap provides a cross-check seam between the inserted image and the existing sequence. The (A4) gap is closed. Downstream: treat (A4)/H_4 as canonical from IMG-026B; treat (A5)/H_5 as a duplicate that appears on BOTH IMG-026B and IMG-027 (Stage 3 should confirm they agree).

Figures 6, 8, and 10 are referenced in the results text (e.g., IMG-024 mentions "Figs. 8 and 10") but are NOT present among the 30 supplied images. These are understood from captions of present figures to be the equivalent-orthotropic-shell comparison plots for Shells 1, 2, 3. Their absence is a gap in the image set, not necessarily a paper defect. Flagged so Stage 2 does not assume they were extracted.

Several Appendix denominators/exponents are small and may be mis-read at this resolution (e.g., possible "km^4" vs "k_m^4" rendering in IN4/IN10 on IMG-029). These are NOT classified here; they are left for Stage 2 transcription and Stage 3 audit. Listed only as a readability caution.

image.apparent_page: title page (page number not clearly visible; "Received 6 January 1969" and codes 12.7,12.3 at top)
image.content_type: title / authors / affiliation / abstract
image.readability: CLEAR
image.defect_notes: Abstract has highlighter marking over some sentences; text still legible. Small font for affiliation.

image.content_type: symbol/notation definition list (two columns)
image.readability: CLEAR
image.continuation_status: continues next (symbol list runs onto IMG-003)
image.defect_notes: Dense two-column symbol list; small italic subscripts. Useful for cross-check against notation_register.md but not to be extracted as theory in Stage 1.

image.content_type: symbol list tail (nu_w, sigma, chi, omega) + subscript key (w,r,st / s,theta,s-theta)
image.readability: CLEAR
image.continuation_status: continues previous (tail of IMG-002 symbol list)
image.defect_notes: Large amount of whitespace; only a few rows of content. No defects.

image.content_type: narrative prose (literature background, motivation)
image.readability: CLEAR
image.defect_notes: Single-column body text. Contains reference superscripts. No defects.

image.content_type: narrative prose (scope/method overview of the present study)
image.readability: CLEAR
image.defect_notes: Body text only. No defects.

image.figure_numbers_visible: references Fig. 1 in text (figure itself not on this image)
image.content_type: theory equations (wall strains, membrane strains, curvature changes) + framing text
image.readability: CLEAR
image.defect_notes: Equations well printed. Some highlighter on words ("strains", "distance z", "extensional strains", "circumferential"). Numerator/denominator stacks in Eqs (6),(8),(9) are small - flag for careful Stage 2 transcription.

image.figure_numbers_visible: references Fig. 2 in text (figure not on this image)
image.content_type: theory text (curvature/displacement conventions, linear shell theory assumption, thin-stiffener assumption) + ring/stringer strain equations
image.readability: CLEAR (verified via 2.2x upscale; equation numbers and ring/stringer strain forms confirmed)
image.defect_notes: Original render slightly soft; upscaled copy stored at logs/_tmp_zoom/B7_full.png for Stage-1 reading aid. Highlighter on several phrases.

image.content_type: stringer twist equation (13) + theory text introducing equivalent-orthotropic vs general approaches
image.readability: PARTIAL (Eq. 13 top line has small stacked fractions and subscripts; surrounding text legible but compressed)
image.defect_notes: Top equation (13) is the most cramped on the page; recommend Stage 2 transcribe at high zoom. Highlighter present.

image.figure_numbers_visible: Fig. 1 (caption: "Thin truncated conical shell - geometry and coordinate system")
image.content_type: strain-energy expression Eq (14) (equivalent-orthotropic approach), rigidity definitions Eq (15), and Fig. 1 line diagram (bottom-left) with reference footnotes
image.readability: CLEAR for equations; Fig. 1 line art CLEAR but small; footnote citations small
image.defect_notes: Eq (15) is a tall braced list of rigidity definitions (D_w, D_r, D_st, S_r, S_st, K_w, K_r, K_st, T_r, T_st) - small type, Stage 2 to transcribe carefully. Fig. 1 shares the page (lower-left) with text.

image.content_type: unnumbered relation b_st = s*beta*cos(alpha) at top; strain-energy expression Eq (16) for the general reinforced-shell approach (double-integral wall term + sum over P rings + sum over Q stringers)
image.readability: CLEAR (large multi-line equation, well printed)
image.defect_notes: Eq (16) spans several lines with summation limits P and Q and subscripted brackets ]_r and ]_st - Stage 2 must preserve grouping and limits. Highlighter on one word.

image.content_type: rigidity definitions Eq (17) (general approach: D_r,D_st,S_r,S_st,K_r,K_st,T_r,T_st), kinetic energy Eq (18) (equivalent orthotropic), mass terms Eq (19), kinetic energy Eq (20) (general), mass terms Eq (21)
image.readability: CLEAR (single column, equations legible; subscripts small)
image.defect_notes: Five numbered equations on one page; Eq (17) is a braced definition list. Highlighter on "kinetic energy". Dense but legible.

image.figure_numbers_visible: Fig. 2 (caption: "Element of reinforced conical shell"; labels STRINGER, THIN WALL, RING)
image.content_type: boundary conditions Eq (22) (simply supported ends), trial displacement functions Eqs (23)-(25) (u,v,w after Grigolyuk), wavenumber Eq (26), harmonic time dependence Eq (27); Fig. 2 isometric line drawing at bottom
image.readability: CLEAR for equations and figure labels
image.defect_notes: Eqs (23)-(25) carry the "s^2 sin k_m(s-s1)" amplitude form - note for Stage 2 the s^2 factor and direction labels. Highlighter on several phrases (simply supported, radial direction, m/n definitions). Fig. 2 shares page.

image.equation_numbers_visible: none (unnumbered geometric relation M=(s2-s1) appears as a dimension label)
image.content_type: engineering drawing: cross-section sketch (labels m, w, h, k, beta), elevation with d, D, b_r/2, b_r, M=(s2-s1), section A-A, and assembly callouts (1/4 in. aluminum end plate, adjustable nuts, 1/2 in. threaded steel rod 20 threads/in.)
image.readability: PARTIAL (line drawing clear; small dimension labels m,w,h,k near the cross-section are tiny and relevant to notation conflicts NC005/NC006 - read with care in Stage 2)
image.defect_notes: Wide landscape figure with whitespace. Small section labels (A, w-like, h, k) are exactly the symbols flagged in the notation conflict ledger - mark any unclear ones as ambiguous in Stage 2, do not guess.
image.rescan_needed: false (legible, but Stage 2 should zoom the cross-section labels)

image.content_type: 3x3 frequency matrix eigenvalue equation Eq (28) (H_1..H_9, symmetric, times {u_mn,v_mn,w_mn} = 0), characteristic cubic Eq (29) (A_3 X^3 - A_2 X^2 + A_1 X - A_0 = 0), coefficient definitions Eq (30) (A_0,A_1,A_2,A_3), with X = omega^2 = (2 pi f)^2; text "the H_i are given in the Appendix"
image.readability: PARTIAL (matrix and cubic-coefficient subscripts are small; the 3x3 matrix entries and the A_i definitions need high-zoom transcription in Stage 2; sign pattern of Eq (29) must be preserved exactly)
image.defect_notes: Critical equations for the eigenproblem. Sign convention of the cubic (alternating signs) and the (2*pi*f)^2 grouping are audit-sensitive. Highlighter on a phrase about "combination of m and n".

image.content_type: generalized-displacement (mode ratio) solutions: Eq (31) u_mn in terms of v_mn,w_mn; Eq (32) v_mn in terms of w_mn; Eq (33) normalization w_mn = 1.0
image.readability: CLEAR (large, well-spaced equations)
image.defect_notes: Eq (32) has nested brackets and omega^2 H_7/H_9 terms - preserve grouping in Stage 2. No image defects.

image.table_numbers_visible: references Tables I and II in text
image.content_type: narrative: description of three test shells (Shell 1: three rings; Shell 2: three rings + three stringers; Shell 3: three rings + six stringers), fabrication (6061 aluminum), test setup
image.readability: CLEAR
image.defect_notes: Numbered list of shell configurations highlighted. Body text legible.

image.content_type: data table: rows D, d, M, alpha, t_w, P, b_r, j, h, Q, beta, m, k for Shell No. 1/2/3
image.readability: CLEAR (clean typeset table, all numeric values legible)
image.defect_notes: Table I uses symbols m, j, k, h as GEOMETRIC dimensions - these collide with modal m, imaginary j, etc. (notation conflicts NC002/NC007). Record values verbatim in Stage 2; do not reconcile with modal symbols.

image.content_type: data table: shell wall (6061 Al), rings (6061 Al), stringers (2024 Al) with E, nu/G, gamma values
image.readability: CLEAR
image.defect_notes: Three-column property table; values match notation_register Table II references. No defects.

image.apparent_page: end of Experimental / start of Results
image.section_heading: II. EXPERIMENTAL (tail) + III. RESULTS AND DISCUSSION (heading)
image.figure_numbers_visible: references figures in text (e.g., Fig. 5-class comparisons)
image.content_type: narrative: end of test-procedure description, then Results-and-Discussion opening prose (natural-frequency determination, n ranges)
image.readability: CLEAR
image.defect_notes: Section boundary occurs mid-page (II ends, III begins). Body text legible.

image.figure_numbers_visible: Fig. 4 (caption: "Conical shell reinforced with three rings and six stringers")
image.content_type: photograph of an instrumented test shell (Shell 3) on the test fixture
image.readability: PARTIAL (halftone photograph; caption legible, but photographic detail is low-contrast / dark)
image.defect_notes: Photograph carries no theory data; included for completeness. Caption confirmed. No rescan needed for theory purposes.

image.figure_numbers_visible: Fig. 5 (caption: "Comparison of theory (general reinforced shell) with experiment (Shell No. 1)")
image.content_type: x-y plot: Frequency (kHz) vs Number of Circumferential Waves n; legend keyed by number of meridional half-waves (theory vs experiment markers); plus discussion prose
image.readability: PARTIAL (axis labels, legend and caption CLEAR; individual plotted data-point values are not precisely readable - this is a graph, not a numeric table)
image.defect_notes: Figure values must NOT be digitized/estimated in Phase 1 (CSV/data comparison forbidden). Record only labels and what the curves represent.

image.figure_numbers_visible: Fig. 7 (left; "Comparison of theory (general reinforced shell) with experiment (Shell No. 2)") and Fig. 9 (right; "...with experiment (Shell No. 3)") - both captions confirmed by zoom
image.content_type: two x-y comparison plots (Shell 2 and Shell 3) side by side, each Frequency vs n; surrounding discussion prose
image.readability: PARTIAL (captions/axes CLEAR after zoom; data-point values not precisely readable)
image.defect_notes: This single image holds TWO figures (Fig. 7 and Fig. 9). Note that Figs 6 and 8 (equivalent-orthotropic comparisons for Shells 1 and 2) are NOT present in the image set - see manifest flag MF-002. Do not digitize plot data in Phase 1.

image.figure_numbers_visible: Fig. 11 (caption: "Example of nodal pattern for mode of vibration")
image.content_type: developed (fan/sector) nodal map: node lines (dashed), X = shaker location, r = ring, st = stringer; annotated Shell 1, m = 1 or 2, n = 7 or 6, f = 3514 cps; axes in inches and degrees
image.readability: PARTIAL (overall figure CLEAR; small embedded labels n=..., m=..., and grid annotations are tiny)
image.defect_notes: Marker "X" here is the SHAKER location, NOT eigenvalue X (notation conflict NC004). Preserve that meaning. Numeric f and mode labels are figure annotations only.

image.content_type: narrative discussion: nodal-pattern interpretation, equivalent-orthotropic theory shown inadequate (Figs 8 & 10), double natural frequencies at n = 3,6,9, interference between modes, Weingarten comparison
image.readability: CLEAR (two-column body text)
image.defect_notes: Text references Figs 6/8/10 which are absent from the image set (flag MF-002). Theory-relevant qualitative statements (which approach fails, where double roots occur) should be captured by Stage 2 as modeling-relevant text.

image.figure_numbers_visible: Fig. 12 (caption: "Example of nodal pattern for mode of vibration")
image.content_type: developed (fan/sector) nodal map: node lines (dashed), X = shaker location, r = ring, st = stringer; annotated Shell 3, m = 1, n = 9, f = 5090 cps; "response" arrows; axes in inches and degrees
image.readability: PARTIAL (figure CLEAR; small embedded annotations tiny)
image.defect_notes: Same X = shaker-location caveat as Fig. 11 (NC004). Figure annotations only; no theory equations.

image.section_heading: Appendix A. Components in the Frequency Matrix of Eq. 28
image.content_type: appendix equations: H_1 (A1), H_2 (A2), H_3 (A3) expressed via IN1..IN16 shorthand, with D_w,S_w,K_w,T_w and starred variants, k_m, sin/cos alpha factors
image.readability: PARTIAL (long multi-line expressions; many small superscripts/subscripts and starred IN* terms - high-zoom transcription required in Stage 2)
image.defect_notes: Wide landscape equations. THIS PAGE ENDS AT (A3). The (A4)/H_4 that follows is now supplied by inserted IMG-026B (MF-001 RESOLVED in r2); IMG-027 resumes at (A5). Heavy use of IN* (starred) shorthand whose definitions appear on IMG-029/030.

image.original_filename: (user-supplied in r2; see source_note for required on-disk filename)
image.apparent_paper_order: 26.5 (logically between IMG-026 and IMG-027 in the Appendix A sequence)
image.apparent_page: Appendix A (the page/region carrying H_4 and the top of H_5)
image.section_heading: Appendix A (continued) - Components in the Frequency Matrix of Eq. 28
image.content_type: appendix equations: H_4 (A4) - the previously-missing coefficient - and H_5 (A5). H_4 (A4): D_w,D_r*,D_st*,nu_w D_w terms with cos alpha factors, bracketed [4(IN2)-4 k_m(IN14)+k_m^2(IN10)] and [4(IN1)-4 k_m(IN13)+k_m^2(IN9)](IN*16) groups, plus a (1-nu_w)/2 * D_w * pi/cos alpha * (IN2) * n^2 term. H_5 (A5): D_w,D_r*,nu_w D_w sin alpha terms, an S_r* cos alpha bracket containing (IN*12)(2 - n^2/cos^2 alpha)+k_m(IN*2), and an S_st* cos alpha braced group {4(IN11)+k_m[8(IN1)-2(IN7)]-6 k_m^2(IN13)+k_m^3(IN9)}(IN*15).
image.readability: CLEAR (user-supplied crop is high-contrast; equation numbers (A4) and (A5), starred IN* terms, exponents and denominators are legible)
image.continuation_status: INSERTED: continues IMG-026 (which ends at A3) and precedes IMG-027 (which starts at A5). Overlaps IMG-027 by one equation: (A5)/H_5 appears on BOTH IMG-026B and IMG-027.
image.defect_notes: No image defect. Note the deliberate (A5) overlap with IMG-027 - this is a duplicate region, not a conflict; Stage 3 should verify the two renderings of H_5 agree. The unique/new content of this image is (A4)/H_4.
image.source_note: PROVENANCE: This image was supplied by the user in conversation revision r2 to fill the (A4) gap (manifest flag MF-001). It was pasted inline in chat rather than uploaded as a file, so at the time of this manifest update NO source PNG exists on disk under 00_inputs/images/batch_F/. REQUIRED ACTION FOR FILE-ONLY HANDOFF: save the supplied screenshot to "00_inputs/images/batch_F/IMG-00 (26B).png" so Stage 2/Stage 3 can read it as a file. Until that file is placed, IMG-026B's canonical visual source is the inline chat image; the manifest metadata above already reflects its verified content.

image.content_type: appendix equations: H_5 (A5) and H_6 (A6) expressed via IN/IN* shorthand with D_w,S_w,K_w,T_w terms
image.readability: PARTIAL (long multi-line expressions; small sub/superscripts - high-zoom required)
image.continuation_status: continues previous; continues next. NOTE (r2): the (A4) gap before this page is now filled by inserted IMG-026B. IMG-026B also repeats this page's first equation (A5)/H_5 (one-equation overlap seam).
image.defect_notes: Left-margin start confirmed by zoom as "H_5 = D_w pi sin a (IN13...". The (A4)/H_4 that was absent between IMG-026 and IMG-027 is now supplied by IMG-026B (MF-001 RESOLVED). (A5)/H_5 appears on both IMG-026B and this image - Stage 3 should confirm the two agree. Audit-sensitive page.
image.rescan_needed: false (A4 recovered via IMG-026B; this page is otherwise readable)

image.content_type: appendix mass-coefficient equations: H_7 (A7), H_8 (A8), H_9 (A9) in terms of m_w, m_r*, m_st* and IN/IN* shorthand with cos alpha factors
image.readability: CLEAR (each is a single line; relatively well printed)
image.defect_notes: These three are the cleanest appendix equations. Note starred mass terms (m_r*, m_st*) and IN9/IN10/IN3/IN4 with IN*15/IN*16 - Stage 2 to preserve star placement exactly.

image.equation_numbers_visible: (A10); plus the (A11) block of IN definitions (IN1..IN16) for the equivalent orthotropic shell - block referenced as "Eqs. A11" on IMG-030
image.content_type: guidance text on starred (*) substitution / two approaches; stringer-property definitions Eq (A10) (D_st,S_st,K_st,T_st,m_st for equivalent orthotropic shell); definitions of IN1 through IN15 (and "and" leading into IN16) as polynomial expressions in s1,s2,k_m
image.readability: PARTIAL (verified via vertical-half zoom; IN definitions legible but small fractions/exponents - e.g., possible "k_m^4" vs "km^4" rendering in IN4/IN10 - flagged MF-003 for Stage 2/3)
image.continuation_status: continues previous; the IN-definition list continues onto IMG-030 (IN16)
image.defect_notes: Portrait page, dense. The (A11) equation label is not printed beside every IN line; it is the equivalent-orthotropic IN block (confirmed by IMG-030 text "obtained from Eqs. A11"). Stage 2 to zoom each IN line.
image.rescan_needed: false (legible at zoom; flag exponents for audit)

image.content_type: general-reinforced-shell IN definitions Eq (A12): summation forms over P rings / Q stringers for the starred exceptions (IN2,IN4,IN6,IN7,IN8,IN10,IN12,IN13) and IN15/IN16 (each with two alternative cos^2/sin^2 forms); closing appendix text (theta_1 = 0; rotatory inertia, transverse shear, structural damping neglected; rings share material/section, stringers share material/section, but shell/ring/stringer materials may differ; "In Eqs. A1-A8 ...")
image.readability: PARTIAL (verified via two-half zoom; summation expressions legible; small exponents)
image.continuation_status: continues previous (closes Appendix A and the document portion provided)
image.defect_notes: Closing text states key global modeling assumptions - capture in Stage 2 as modeling-relevant text. Note explicit reference "In Eqs. A1-A8" reinforces that the H-set runs A1..A9 with A4 missing from the images (MF-001).

completeness_check.all_images_listed: true (30 original IMG-001..IMG-030 + inserted IMG-026B = 31 catalogued)
completeness_check.no_extraction_performed: true (only headings, numbers, content-type, readability, continuation, defects recorded; equation content noted for IMG-026B is descriptive cataloguing to justify the insertion, not Stage-2 transcription)
completeness_check.notation_register_used_for: symbol-awareness only (to flag notation-conflict-sensitive labels), not for extraction
completeness_check.appendix_equation_coverage: A1,A2,A3 (IMG-026); A4,A5 (IMG-026B); A5,A6 (IMG-027); A7,A8,A9 (IMG-028); A10 + IN(A11) block (IMG-029); A12 (IMG-030). H-series A1..A9 now complete.
completeness_check.open_items_for_later_stages: MF-001 RESOLVED (A4/H_4 supplied via IMG-026B). MF-002 OPEN (Figs 6/8/10 absent) - per user r2 directive, frequency/comparison plots are NOT required for the current objective and need not be analyzed unless they carry equations/definitions/table values; this flag is therefore informational only. MF-003 OPEN (small Appendix exponents to verify in Stage 2/3). ACTION ITEM: place the IMG-026B source PNG on disk at "00_inputs/images/batch_F/IMG-00 (26B).png" to complete the file-only handoff (see IMG-026B/source_note).
